name: ExtensionMeasure_SupplementalData_LinkId
title: R5: Unique id for supplementalData in measure (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Measure.supplementalData.linkId` 0..1 `string`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` has a context of Measure.supplementalData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Unique id for supplementalData in measure (new)
element.comment:
Element `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` has a context of Measure.supplementalData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.supplementalData.linkId` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This ''can'' be a meaningful identifier (e.g. a LOINC code) but is not intended to have any meaning.  GUIDs or sequential numbers are appropriate here.

LinkIds can have whitespaces and slashes by design. Tooling should not rely on linkIds being valid XHTML element IDs, and should not directly embed them as such

element.short: Unique id for supplementalData in measure
element.definition: An identifier that is unique within the Measure allowing linkage to the equivalent item in a MeasureReport resource.
element.comment:
This ''can'' be a meaningful identifier (e.g. a LOINC code) but is not intended to have any meaning.  GUIDs or sequential numbers are appropriate here.

LinkIds can have whitespaces and slashes by design. Tooling should not rely on linkIds being valid XHTML element IDs, and should not directly embed them as such
